analysis.name: Alexander Francis
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 26 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: This class was awful.  He is unhelpful as well as a terrible test maker.  His class ended up with a giant unspoken curve at the end so he could adjust the grades due to us all doing so poorly.  If you have to take this class good luck.  And his acoustics class is worse if anything.

It is obvious the professor knows what he is talking about. He is incredibly knowledgeable in linguistics; however, he does not frequently lecture about things relevant to what is being tested. The TAs are the only reason anyone knows what to do. He can be an excellent resource but does not help much during lecture.

The class was challenging but I felt like he was very understanding and worked with the class. He adjusted his grading when we were all doing bad. There are some topics that are hard but I think his lectures plus the textbook explained them pretty well. I really enjoyed this class and Dr. Francis is an interesting guy. Talk to him outside of class!
The homework and quizzes are unrelated to what you're working on in class, prof gets off topic everyday and is unwilling to meet with students when needed.

Dr. Francis made this class way harder than it had to be. I feel like I know absolutely nothing about phonetics. I do not feel prepared for grad school. He even admits Praat is not going to be significant for us in our careers, but the whole class revolves around Praat. It's awful. Take with someone else if you can.

analysis.name: Andrew Freed
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 292 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Honestly my favorite professor at Purdue. He is always so excited about what he teaches and goes through so much effort to show us cool examples. I love how you can ask him questions about anything EAPS related on Hotseat. I have learned and enjoyed this class so much because of him.
This guy is awesome. He keeps class interesting and tells you what is exactly on each exam. If every teacher was like him I would gladly go to class everyday!

I loved taking this class - it was such a nice break from my other ones . Attendance isn't mandatory, but he does give in class participation quizzes  a lot. There isn't really any reason not to go to lecture, anyway - he makes it very entertaining. Don't bother buying the textbook - all the homework questions can be googled.
Easy A class, and enough extra credit, many announcement and email to remind you to remember the deadline. Patience!

He is an amazing professor and an even better person! I cannot rave about Prof. Freed enough. Whatever you do, PLEASE take his class. He has some of the most entertaining and engaging lectures and is so kind and caring to students.

analysis.name: Andrew Toms
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 95 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Only thing that could make him better would be if he was right handed
Wore a pickachu hat to lecture once you could say he's a pretty cool guy. Most of the time his lectures on last about 20 minutes.

Toms is a pretty funny/entertaining guy.  He always throws in some jokes or goes on a funny rant during lectures.  He also writes the exams to be very easy.  His teaching skills are just average in my opinion.  He uses the same examples in lecture that are used in the textbook and tends not to fully explain the key concepts.

Doesn't waste any time. Gives you exactly what you need for exams. He wasn't even my prof and I went to all his lectures anyway outside of the first week. He's also hilarious in a kind of out of pocket way, which is awesome.

Solid lecturer, but can go too fast at times and the class information is tough. Something this professor could do better is repeating important information. Stories were funny, and made the lectures better.

analysis.name: Andrei Gabrielov
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 79 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Gabrielov's like that sweet old grandad you have. The kind tries to teach you things but in the end you don't really know what he's saying and end up smiling and nodding at everything. Now imagine that grandad being Russian. Needless to say, I didn't understand much of what he said and just like at family dinners, I excused myself and left.

Basically the wacky uncle of the Math department. He seems like a very friendly person, but he skips steps in example problems, is very difficult to understand (thick accent), and doesn't explain new material very well. He may also be a Soviet sleeper cell, but that's based more on suspicion than evidence.

As lecturer, Gabrielov does not excel. He has a moderately thick russian accent, and when combined with a small amount of background noise and a low quality microphone he can be hard to understand in lecture halls.  His strengths lie in face to face interactions. He is extremely intelligent and resolve any issues you have with course material.

Absolutely terrible. So hard to understand, skips steps all the time, and writes really hard tests. Going to class with Gabrielov isn't even worth it. I survived through using the internet, but it was not a good experience. Avoid taking at all costs.

His handwriting is very bad, he reuses paper so you can see work on the other side, his accent is difficult at times, his lectures have NO structure whatsoever and he blames students for his own mistakes and says 'you need to watch what I'm doing and tell me if I make a mistake'. If you get him, watch other lectures on BoilerCast. Good luck...

analysis.name: Andres Vargas
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 114 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: He 's very helpful in class and at office hours. You do not need a textbook if you attend lectures and use slides. You should probably practice his problem sets a lot, I didn't realize this till the final, so don't be like me. Hotseat points boost grades ideally by a letter grade and his exams are pretty difficult since there are no sample exams.

Take his class, seriously! A good professor, very helpful !!!!!! His lectures and slides are easy to understand and helpful enough. No need to buy the textbook. And also, he took participation points. Exams are moderate, and getting A's for exams is not that hard. It's all multiple choice questions. If you do all the homework, it's A in your hand!

Vargas is an incredible professor. In addition to teaching very well, he uses third-party elements (e.g., news clips) to enhance his lectures. The class is not difficult; he gives extra credit for merely showing up to class (very generous), and the hw/practices exams are exactly like the exams  very approachable guy & knowledgeable.

Dr Vargas is one of the greatest economics professors and lectures I've had! he is super kind and very helpful. His lectures are well organized with lots of examples and always willing to answer questions before, during, and after class. I've had him for macro too and although the topics can be quiet hard he can help you through understanding well!

This was an ok professor, but his lectures are basically him reading presentations. He doesn't make content easy, and I would learn the same amount of things through the textbook or youtube. He says attendance isn't mandatory, but uses iClicker for points. Not the best if you want to be genuinely engaged during lectures.

analysis.name: Adrian Wolanski
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 19 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: My GOAT. Professor Wolanski was amazing. I actually did better here than in MGMT 30301 (which is basically AP Stats). He's very available in office hours, quick with emails, funny, and genuinely cares about Purdue (he's from Indiana). His engaging style encourages collaboration and discussion, so you're basically studying while learning.

Got an A in this class and a C in 30301. The content in this class was harder, but Wolanski makes it easier to understand. He is very caring and really wants you to do good. Go to his office hours and get help on homework it makes the class a lot easier. Do not wait to do the homework last minute it takes a lot of time. He is an amazing professor!

He is such an amazing prof. He really cares about every single student he has. He makes sure that he know everyone's name and gets to know them. I would say go to office hours because homework can be challenging. Another piece of advice I would give is to make sure you to to lecture because he goes over everything that you need to know.

He knows what he is doing, and he tries his best to make it as easy as it can be while not simplifying too much. Homework takes a while, but they're just once weekly. Exams are OK, 15 multiple choice questions don't sound bad, but you have to know the material, just like every other class. Not a class to stress about, which I liked.

I hate stats so I dreaded this class but Prof Wolanski made up for it. He is very into what he teaches and wants to see his students succeed. He knows that this class is a pathway course for a lot of classes and thats the only reason students take it so he tries to make everything as painless as possible.

analysis.name: George Adams
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 143 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: His test's do not test you over what you have learned or studied for. The material is fairly easy, so he likes to word the questions so they are all impossible to read or comprehend.

By far the most overrated professor at Purdue. Don't understand why so many people like him. Only his delivery is good, his content and exams suck. He doesn't slow down at all and his lectures are quite frankly hard to understand. He assumes that everyone is as passionate as him and thinks everyone understands hardware the same way he does.
His slides is full of information and exams are not related to what was covered in class.

Really passionate about what he teaches, but because of his EE background, he goes way too in-depth on topics and rambles a lot during class. Exams are extremely hard even if you study and class has over 1000 slides for the final exam.  Too much material crammed into one course imo which makes retaining knowledge on what was taught difficult.

While Prof. Adams is clearly passionate about what he teaches and is a generally good lecturer, he has by far some of the WORST tests I have ever seen. Focuses on testing whether you remember obscure trivia rather than if you truly understand the material. Furthermore, the tests are structured to be ambiguous and an english comprehension test.

analysis.name: Cari Ann Boersma
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 17 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Professor Boersma is awesome. She makes an effort to foster a community in the class. You will become a much better speaker by the end of the class. As long as you participate and put effort into the assignments, you will do well. One of the most fun classes I've taken at this school.
She gave good feedback on my assignments and understood us as students. Also was very welcoming and everyone in my class liked her.

She made this class so fun by fostering a very nice community within the class. It was my favorite one this semester! Very presentation based, and you become way more comfortable presenting in front of the class by the end with feedback from her and your classmates. Just follow her instructions, and you'll be all set.

She is a very good COM teacher. She makes everything that we learn very relatable. The class focuses more on presentations with feedback from your peers. The class is pretty laid-back as long as you do what is required of you. She makes the class very fun!

Cari runs an easy yet fair class. as long as you do the quizzes and prepare a little for presentation you can get a A. The class atmosphere is lively, and I looked forward to going to class every day.

analysis.name: Andy Hudmon
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: lectures are very easy to understand. you will leave lecture feeling like you understand what is going on instead of studying for hours trying to figure out what the bullet points are saying. very concise and engaging

Dr. Hudmon is the one redeemable factor in this mess of a class. He works hard to make sure we are understanding his material, which can be difficult at times. However, his exams are extremely fair in comparison to Krusemark's and he pulls directly from the slides. Exam review sessions are helpful, given he actually answers questions unlike others.

Hudmon seems to really care about his students and explains lecture in way for us to understand. His exam questions come straight from the slides or iClicker questions. He emphasized important information that we needed to know (like for homework or exams). He rarely fell behind in slides and if he ever did, he made extra videos to compensate.

Dr. Hudmon is the only reason this class is bearable. His lectures are much easier to follow and he takes time to ensure we are understanding the material. Exam content is pulled straight from the slides and he will even recycle past exam questions. If he was the course coordinator rather than Krusemark, student performance would be way better.

Dr. Hudmon is SO much better than Dr. Krusemark. Don't be discouraged by the horror that is exam one, once you reach Hudmon's content things become a lot more clear. Dr. Hudmon is extremely knowledgeable and seems to care a lot more about his impact on students, he will tell you the key takeaways you need to know for the exams and future courses.

analysis.name: Konstantinos Ameranis
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Ameranis is goated! I found value in his lectures, and his style of teaching is very open and flexible. The accent was a bit hard to understand at times, but otherwise no complaints. Exams were tricky, but nicely curved. Do get started early on the projects tho. Also, his lectures on graphs & hashing were some of the best ive ever seen

Professor Ameranis speaks clearly. Even though he is "new" at teaching, he gets to the point in lectures, he is not monotone or repetitive which makes the 50 minute lectures a breeze. If you have never programmed in your life, well lets just say you will need to put in a lot more effort than those kids who have been programming since 6th grade.

He's a new professor, but by no means unexperienced. I really enjoyed the setup of the lectures, as the concepts explained in class clicked very quickly apposed to if I had to miss a class and learn the information outside of class. 

Main thing: If you show up to classes, put in effort in class, and think critically you will do well.

He was a great professor even though it was his first time teaching the class. His lectures are engaging and he will ask the class questions throughout lecture. He is very accessible at his office hours and he was always very helpful at answering questions.

TA guidance also conflicted with grading.

Poorly organized, hard to follow. She had 1 assginment in Excel and 2 in Tableau. The course is a TABLEAU class, there shouldn't be Excel assignments! Spacing between due dates was bad. GRILLED project presentation groups in front of all students. Her grading was inconsistent, there was hidden grading criteria which many people got points off for.
Incredibly difficult to understand and grades unfairly. Uncooperative with students and refuses to help push students in the right direction.
She does not know how to teach, consistent issues and wrong queries/data taught.
professor is not good at explaining things in the lecture and isnt very engaging

analysis.name: Becky Bokrand
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: She gives amazing bullet points on what to study for exams. The lectures are super boring, but that is more so accounting than her. Dr. Bokrand is super kind and passionate about what she does. She gives great resources to succeed, and extra credit here and there. The class is an easy GPA booster, even if you have trouble staying awake.

Dr. Bokrand was a great professor and a lecturer who wasn't boring to listen to for the 50-minute class time. There were reviews and homework for each chapter learned, and there are 3 total exams (including the final). The exams are difficult if you don't understand the material.

I personally didnt find the class very interesting, but Dr. Bokrand was very passionate and knowledgeable about the topic. Additionally, she was very nice about extra credit, which really helps your grade as the exams are not easy. This class is straight forward, but you need to put in the effort to see the results.

I took Dr. Bokrand's accounting class and thought she was a good professor. Some material was confusing at first, but her explanations and review days helped a lot. Homework was challenging but useful, and exams reflected what she reviewed.

Great professor, the notes style is not the most useful. Provided is fill in the blank notes. I believe handwriting them is better. Be sure to use the book and the practice provided for exam prep. Otherwise, a memorization heavy class.

analysis.name: Josh Boyd
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 113 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: COM 204 is a challenging course, but really, if you go to class, follow the rubric and his grading scale closely, and consult Dr. Boyd and his TA's for help, you will succeed. Dr. Boyd is honest in his grading and his words, and wants his students to grow; however, a level of commitment and consistency is expected for this class.

While the class was okay I found myself losing some sanity over it. Every assignment is graded out of five. While the rubric is clear, it is very objective and opinion based. Major papers are graded on the five-point scale and then multiplied by 6 to get your grade out of 30. Same for the exams. DO NOT use Grammarly; he considers it AI.

This class was difficult, but if you put in genuine effort you can get a good grade! Go to office hours on both major papers! I got a poor grade on the first paper, but still managed to get an A in the class, so don't give up. Don't ever skip lectures, no excuses. He does not post anything online! This being said, his class will teach you a lot.

This class is ridiculously nitpicky. I understand the need to teach students the importance of clarity when it comes to writing, but the way each assignment is graded makes it nearly impossible to succeed. Every assignment is graded out of 5 and, oftentimes, the grading is subjective under the guise of an objective, "clear rubric".
Incredibly tough grader. He uses a 5-point scale for grading and if you meet all requirements on the rubric, you get a 3. Good luck, it's rough.

analysis.name: Bettina Klose
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 36 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: This professor isn't just a "tough grader". ECON 340 is essentially a math class, but you can complete all of your math perfectly and receive a C if your work can't "teach another student how to do the work as well". You're supposed to write a textbook for every assignment, and feedback is vague or nonexistent. TAs were bad, too. Avoid if you can.

I had Dr.Klose for Game Theory (ECON 451) and she was fine. I was worried with the bad reviews, but most of the content was not hard to follow. Guessing that most of the reviews are from an earlier period. The exams were difficult, but we were told what to study. This course was more difficult than most but not the hardest I have ever taken.

Poorly designed course. She lets the curve do all the work. You will pass the class as long as you aren't the very bottom. Her grading is the most unreasonable and inconsistent I've ever seen out of all my years of undergrad, graduate and even high school. This is the first and only time I've cried because of class. Avoid at all costs if possible.

Bad lectures, I would ask for help with something, and she seemingly wouldn't read the full question before responding with a nonhelpful answer. Prepare to teach yourself. I repeatedly had to go and protest against her grading mistakes she made in grading to give me my correct score. I should not have to review the professors grading ever.

Dr. Klose is lecture-heavy. Her entire class is composed of going over material in a pretty bland way. She's unclear on how much work to show during problem sets and exams. Exams are pretty tight on time.

analysis.name: Craig Brown
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 24 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: The rest of the reviews are very telling for Professor Brown. I am suprised Purdue admin hasnt seen this and looked into his teaching style. Basically, he teaches by going at 2x speed through things he expects that you know to teach complex topics. He does not blend the theory and math very well together and makes things very difficult. dont. take.

lectures are very confusing and hard to follow and do not aid you properly for the exams that he writes.  I genuinely feel like I understood the content when studying for an exam but would give questions on the exam that are nothing to do with what we learned. Brightspace page has no information. Cold calls sometimes and is rude and unapproachable

Professor Brown is extremely disorganized. He puts material on exams that we never learned, and the study guides are useless. No answer keys are posted, and lectures aren't tied to textbook chapters. Homework isn't reopened, so there's no way to review or improve. You'll end up teaching yourself everything just to keep up. Would not recommend.

One of the less enjoyable courses I've taken at Purdue. He does not seem to be trying to help when asked, as previous review mentioned, refuses to show clear grading standard, refuses to give homework and test answers, practice paper is pretty much irrelevant from actual test. Allows cheat sheet, has a take home final that is in the form of essay.
Craig is literally a king. Exams are def hard though. Zoom class on Fridays which was a plus. Can see why people may find him intimidating but he's actually super nice.

analysis.name: Bruno Ribeiro
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 34 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Worst ever. The slides are not well organized and have many mistakes. The homeworks are peculiar and just wasting of time.
He is not a good teacher. English is bad and nonlogical
He is trying to persuade us that he is a good teacher and beg us to connect with him at office hour. But, you are unable to convey us the information that you can solve the issue at class.

Riberio is what happens when a circus clown is asked to teach a 300-level CS course. Horrendous slides have no takeaway material to help you with the homework or exams. No communication with the TAs. Will humbly brag how he asked android to change its facial recognition code several times in the semester. Maybe I should write that on the final.

I don't know what to say. He is passionate and his English is fluent. But his logic is sooo bad that it could make you feel like: what is he doing for the whole class and why am I here. Also, he made too many political jokes and lauged at the joke made by himself, which would be really awkward for international students.

analysis.name: Beatriz Galarza Tohen
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: One of the most caring profs I've had at Purdue. I remember near the end of the semester she told me that my smile genuinely brightened her day. Said at the beginning of the semester that she would always take our side no matter what. Genuine unconditional kindness that you don't see often elsewhere (but should).

ENGR 131 isn't a hard class. It's not too exciting either, but Prof. Galarza did her best to make classes kinda fun or have us practice an engineering soft skill (teamwork). 
Prof. Galarza is one of the nicest, most caring professors at Purdue. She also bumped my A- up to an A because I asked (the FYE 3.2 GPA, iykyk), because I got to know her.

She might be one of the most caring professors at this university. She's always available for her students and she truly wants you to succeed. ENGR 131 wasn't a hard class but I had a 7:30, and I'm really thankful that I had her class because I didn't often dread getting up to go to it. She also gives you a lot of studio time to work on projects.

ENGR 13100 I think is a pretty tedious class because the course fits a lot into a few months. Dr. Galarza made the class so much more tolerable. She is a sweet and supportive teacher and is the first person at Purdue who made me feel like I belonged. She makes effort to know all of her students personally and really cares about their well-being.

Professor Galarza is super caring and always makes sure to asks students how they are doing. She is also very helpful when answering students' questions. The class curriculum itself is slow and tedious though so I would recommend taking ENGR 133 over ENGR 131 if you're taking a lighter course load.

analysis.name: Casey Krusemark
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 38 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Krusemark has to be the worst professor out the pre-pharmacy curriculum. He could never give straightforward answers during exam review sessions and wouldn't know the answers to his own questions. Slideshows are an absolute eye sore and information dump, and somehow everything is important given how hyperspecific and hard his exams are.

If you value your sanity, please take this godforsaken class anywhere else. "Unlike most sports games, you can all win this class!" (quoted directly from his first day analogies), and then proceeds to have exam averages in the low fifties. I am a victim of this man's poor ability to teach and will have nightmares about his exams.

biochem is supposed to be a challenging course, but  not like this. his section of the course makes no sense, and he agrees bc he confuses himself and cant even answer questions from students. he seems like a nice guy, but as a professor... he is terrible. his counterpart in this course may be the only reason why students pass.

It's wild to think he's the course coordinator when he is the reason students don't pass this class. For a course coordinator, he says a lot of "I think" and "maybe"s like he doesn't know what he's talking about (which is true, he doesn't). He's also the type to start the semester with a speech about how everyone can get As and why he won't curve

This class is a joke. Most of your grade will come from how you score on the exams, but there are very few resources to help you succeed, even if you go to all of the lectures and pay attention. Dr. Krusemark is extremely vague about the content breakdown on the exams and refuses to provide answer keys for the practice exams.

analysis.name: Congfang Huang

review_highlights.comment: Classes are monotonous and poorly prepared. The slides are often confusing and overtly abstract for rather simple topics. And, at times, it's very difficult to understand the professor's explanations. Grading is generous, but her teaching style is not. Would not take this class with this professor again.

Congfang genuinely cares about her students, especially when you are willing to discuss with her. However, I DO think Congfang needs a better preparation of the course, especially for teaching students materials in a logical and mutually understandable way. You might have to rely mostly on yourself for the course, although the grading is generous.

Congfang's IE 230 class is made up almost entirely of exam credit. There is barely any material to actively practice the topics covered in lecture. She is very hard to understand, which I know is not a fair criticism but it should still be taken into account. Her slides are just mindless info dumps with little to no analysis of the topics.

Grading Scale is pretty lenient, with an 84 being the minimum grade for an A. Not a lot of material, but it can be difficult to understand. This was her first time teaching, and it was at times difficult to understand. She has good exam reviews. Her review guide is very similar to the exam. She does genuinely care about her students/

The course material itself was fairly easy, but understanding the professor is a bit of a challenge. This was also her first time teaching the course, so maybe it was just a general nervousness or getting used to teaching the material. Prepare to teach yourself the material. Don't let this discourage you from taking this course.

analysis.name: Cecilia Ying
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 20 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: This professor is honestly so bad at teaching and the lectures are completely different from the recitations. The TAs don't know anything about what they're teaching and we're blindly expected to know what to do on the labs. The midterm was bad as well as the professor didn't even talk about 1/3 of the contents on it n now my GPA is suffering.

The lectures have almost nothing to do with the recitations. Lab grades didn't come out for 3 weeks, while we had already submitted another one. Doing iClicker in lecture, 80% of people were getting the questions wrong because they didn't know what she was talking about. I dropped bc I'm CODO'ing to HK & this class was a complete waste of my time.

No idea how she was hired by Purdue. Every other Professor I've had in Daniels is miles better. While the content in class itself was fairly easy, the grading system made no since. 40% of our grade was based of 4 random in class kahoots made from unspecified material. Other than the lab projects there was no hw. Purdue please hire someone else.

Total disconnect between lecture and lab. They feel like two different courses. The professor is detached from the lab, and TAs lack any passion for teaching. The lecture content is disorganized and has zero practical use for the lab. As a business major, this is the biggest waste of tuition at Purdue.

Class has a lot of potential. It's first year of the class(fall sem 2025)but there are flaws. The projects are overall interesting; you make your own chatbot agent for a local biz, good for resume and overall skills. The only coding we did was prompt engineering- we put into Gemini and it generates for us but we interpret. It's alright tbh take it.

analysis.name: Joseph (Kuan-Hua) Chen
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 178 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Holy cow Dr. Chen is great. Actually had a different professor for Calc 3 and I skipped all of his lectures so I could watch Chen's lectures online. Great handwriting, explains things at a reasonable level and pace. Gave me an unreasonable standard to hold the rest of my professors to.

Chenflix saved my life during math courses at purdue (162, 261, 266), and I've heard his in person lectures are even better. Very good at breaking topics down, explaining how to do them, and why we do them. Very efficient teaching style with not a lot of time being wasted too. Very easy to understand, one of the best professors at purdue.

Professor Chen is a great lecturer, not only for his ability to explain mathematical concepts in a clear way, but also his commitment to making his lectures accessible to other students. He does a great job at giving his students a strong intuition about how we model/solve differential equations. This intuition is easily applied to other courses.

Amazing professor. You can easily notice that he hard work that he puts into the class because he genuinely wants to see his students excel. He doesn't make things harder than they have to be. He explains everything thoroughly, clearly and makes even 7:30 classes worth going to.

I do not even go to my professors lectures. I use that free time to watch Chenflix since the way that he explains concepts is very clear. Also since this most math courses til differential equations are standardized if you watch chenflix most lectures are aligned with my professors. His lectures are amazing and I am glad I had him for CALC2.

analysis.name: Meng Cui

10/10

His explanations really made things click, even if the test material was hard

He is by far one of the nicest professors I have ever had. He is extremely accessible outside the class and is very approachable. His teaching pace is also perfect and you will understand everything clearly if you pay attention. He doesnt mind slowing down and repeating concepts should people need him to. Get this professor, you wont regret it!

Such a gem of a prof. Lectures aren't mandatory, but lecture + book  much better understanding. Won't hesitate to re-explain material. Make sure to read the book. Exams are fair, but tough for sure. Most of my respect for him comes from his exam review lectures, which he'll go for as long as needed to go over all the material. Love him

analysis.name: Ryan Umbeck
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 17 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Umbeck is one of the best professors at Purdue. Even during a 7:30am class, he still brought energy and engagement to each lecture that made the content easy to follow and understand. The course material can get a little confusing at times, but Dr. Umbeck goes over everything in great detail and answers questions perfectly.

Making an entry-level statistical inference course interesting is a very difficult task, but Professor Umbeck has a bright and encouraging energy that makes you want to pay attention simply out of respect for the effort he is putting into making the material understandable. Fantastic person, gives great feedback on issues with Stata HW (use him!).

I really enjoyed his class. He does not require attendance yet most of the class still shows up every day because he makes the subject so interesting. I wasn't sure about econ until I took this class.
One of the nicest professors at Purdue and teaches really well because he genuinely cares about the subject.
One of the best professor in Purdue. Always patient to students, answer questions on time.

analysis.name: John Umbeck
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 28 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Teamed up with professor Fisher to team-teach econ and law. This was possibly the worst-run class I have experienced in my time as a student. There was no student support, no specific class schedulecalendar, no exam help, and possibly the worst part of all: full class lectures AND hours upon hours of required deathly boring videos.

He explains complex topics well and respects students. Much more down to earth and approachable than some of the other professors here. He is pretty funny and there is no iclicker or mandatory attendance, yet the class is pretty full which means students actually like hearing him talk.
Really great professor. I would highly recommend to take him if you can. Lectures are short so we he lets you off like 15 minutes early and still manages to teach a very wide array of material.

I do not recommend this class. Law and Economics course is just horrible. There are three exams and those are impossible. If you want to get a bad grade, please take it. There is no help in this class. Just don't take this course. Horrible... just horrible.

Umbeck is clearly a master in his field and makes lectures interesting. I thoroughly enjoyed a lot of the examples that he conveyed to explain different topics of law and economics. The exams are tough, but would still recommend.

analysis.name: Christopher Uyeda
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 36 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Uyeda is an incredible lecturer. He explains things very well and speaks in a clear and precise manner. If you're ever confused he'll make time to sit down with you one on one and he'll take his time with explaining and making sure that you understand. Full of information! He really loves what he teaches and you it shows in his teaching style.

Uyeda is a WONDERFUL professor.  Easily my favorite so far at Purdue.  For someone who absolutely hates chemistry and did sub-par in chem 115/116, I thoroughly enjoyed orgo and did well,  mainly because of this professor.  He goes through each topic and explains the topics so that they are easily understandable, and he cares about his students.

THIS MAN IS JUST WONDERFUL!! First prof ever at Purdue that I have had that actually cares! Always super nice and actually knows how to teach. Gave lecture notes and explain everything clearly. I was shocked how great his class was because of his young age.

The best chem prof! Prepped us well for exams, very approachable in office hours, gave extra credit for iClicker when 3/4 ditched class at 9:30 AM. I scored over 100% on all exams and felt confident before each one (way better than chem129). He is kind + patient when people ask questions and doesn't insult other orgo chem classes (unlike Dr. C).
awesome lecturer, made the concepts super clear, and office hours are SUPER helpful

analysis.name: Valeriia Yatsenko
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 32 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: 310 with Yatsenko is easy if you pay attention and study. Her tests involve plugging numbers into formulas from your cheatsheet. She teaches well, providing examples and iClickers are like exam questions, so there are no surprises. Her reviews shouldn't be this bad.

Professor Yatsenko is a very nice woman. She makes a lot of jokes and overall the classroom environment is positive. However she moves very very very fast and its difficult to even attempt to understand a concept before moving forward to the next.

Honestly one of the worst professors I've had at Purdue. Her attendance policy for 413 was points-based all or nothing (so technically required if you want 10% of your grade). She is hard to hear, she writes on the board but often in places hard to read, and her exams are difficult. The group projects are a mess, too. Avoid if possible!

Please do not take this class if there are other options available. Grade is basically fully decided by final exam. She cannot solve and gets tripped up on her own in class practice questions. Expects you to be able to complete question perfectly after seeing one example that she struggles to solve. Struggles to answer in class questions as well.

Homework + Attendance = 30%, Midterm = 35%, Final Exam = 35%.  Extremely hard exams.  I felt like I could've just read the slides and gotten the same info instead of having to show up to class.  My raw grade for the overall class was a 53.33%, so the class curve was around 25%.  She can be funny sometimes, but overall usually boring.

analysis.name: Vikas Tomar
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: This was a good class, valuable information taught, very reasonable expectation with homeworks/exams, and learned a lot. His teaching is mediocre sometimes, as he likes to brush through certain topics fast with minimal explanations leaving it up to you to understand it to the depth required to do the homeworks. But it's not impossible and doable.
He is very hard. His class is impossible.

Vikas is a pretty basic but good professor. Lectures are sometimes tough to understand but are handwritten, so the pace is easy to follow. Exams are straightforward, with few tricks. He's a pretty chill guy too and keeps things real with the class. Sometimes he will joke during lectures and catch you off guard. Just don't bug him about crib sheets.

He is a good professor but admittedly took some getting used to.  I learned a lot, this class was hard.  The good thing is it counts as a math class.  I would say he is not very good at answering questions in class, either repeating what he already has stated or answering a question not asked.  Apart from that he was a good prof.
Good Professor, really wants to make sure the students learn. Responds quickly to questions.
